description: Benchmark dataset for evaluating Hypothetica pipeline against arXiv literature. 40 cases across 4 domains with originality labels and gold-standard paper sets. Gold set: 9 arXiv rows with title/ID mismatch were removed (see trim list). Duplicate arXiv id 1803.04337 in arxiv_039 removed; arxiv_036 2305.03788 downgraded to supporting. Originality: arxiv_001, arxiv_030, arxiv_033 set to incremental (see benchmark notes).

cases.idea: A federated learning framework where edge clients use structured pruning to reduce local model size before aggregation, and the server reconstructs a full-capacity model using a learned weight interpolation scheme instead of standard FedAvg.

gold_papers.title: FedBE: Making Bayesian Model Ensemble Applicable to Federated Learning
Federated Learning with Matched Averaging
Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data
FedBIAD: Communication-Efficient and Accuracy-Guaranteed Federated Learning with Bayesian Inference-Based Adaptive Dropout

cases.rationale: Combining structured pruning at the client side with learned weight interpolation for reconstruction at the server has not been proposed as a unified system. Existing work treats pruning and aggregation as independent concerns.

cases.idea: A continual learning method that maintains a small 'surprise buffer' — storing only samples that caused the highest gradient conflict with previously learned tasks — and uses these samples as the sole replay set, updated dynamically per task boundary.

gold_papers.title: Gradient Episodic Memory for Continual Learning
Reconstruct before Query: Continual Missing Modality Learning with Decomposed Prompt Collaboration
Dark Experience for General Continual Learning: a Strong, Simple Baseline
Efficient Expansion and Gradient Based Task Inference for Replay Free Incremental Learning

cases.rationale: Gradient conflict as the criterion for buffer sample selection — rather than diversity, recency, or reservoir sampling — is a novel selection strategy not explored in existing replay methods.

cases.idea: A test-time adaptation strategy for vision transformers where patch tokens are selectively masked based on uncertainty estimates from a lightweight auxiliary head, forcing the model to reconstruct context and thereby adapt its representations to distribution shift.

gold_papers.title: Test-Time Training with Masked Autoencoders
MT3: Meta Test-Time Training for Self-Supervised Test-Time Adaption
Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners
Tent: Fully Test-time Adaptation by Entropy Minimization

cases.rationale: Using per-token uncertainty as the masking criterion at test time — rather than random masking — to drive adaptation is a novel formulation bridging uncertainty estimation and self-supervised adaptation.

cases.idea: Applying LoRA-style low-rank adaptation to the value projection matrices of a cross-attention layer in a diffusion model's UNet to enable style transfer with fewer trainable parameters than full fine-tuning.

gold_papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
DreamBooth: Fine Tuning Text-to-Image Diffusion Models for Subject-Driven Generation
Diffusion in the Dark: A Diffusion Model for Low-Light Text Recognition
An Image is Worth One Word: Personalizing Text-to-Image Generation using Textual Inversion

cases.rationale: LoRA has been applied to LLMs and diffusion models broadly; applying it specifically to cross-attention value projections for style is an incremental architectural choice, not a conceptual departure.

cases.idea: Replacing the scalar temperature in softmax attention with a learned per-head temperature vector, allowing different attention heads to operate at different sharpness levels without other changes to the transformer architecture.

gold_papers.title: Attention is All You Need
Do Wide and Deep Networks Learn the Same Things? Uncovering How Neural Network Representations Vary with Width and Depth
cosFormer: Rethinking Softmax in Attention
Talking-Heads Attention

cases.rationale: Per-head temperature scaling is a minor parameterization change. Closely related to learned attention scaling in Talking-Heads and temperature annealing work; the delta over prior art is small.

cases.idea: A curriculum learning schedule for instruction-tuning large language models where training examples are ordered by reward model score (low to high), so the model sees easier instructions first and harder ones later.

gold_papers.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models
Cyclical Curriculum Learning
Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions

cases.rationale: Applying classic curriculum learning to RLHF instruction ordering is a natural extension. The RM-score-as-difficulty proxy has been explored in adjacent data selection literature.

cases.idea: Using a mixture-of-experts routing mechanism where the router is conditioned on the input's language identity token (detected at preprocessing) rather than the input embedding, to improve multilingual LLM performance.

gold_papers.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity
ST-MoE: Designing Stable and Transferable Sparse Expert Models
Efficient Large Scale Language Modeling with Mixtures of Experts
Uni-MoE: Scaling Unified Multimodal LLMs with Mixture of Experts

cases.rationale: Language-conditioned routing is a sensible extension to MoE multilingual work; similar ideas appear in language-specific expert allocation literature.

cases.idea: A knowledge distillation method where a large teacher model's intermediate layer activations are used to supervise a smaller student model, in addition to the final output logits, improving the student's feature representations.

gold_papers.title: Distilling the Knowledge in a Neural Network
FitNets: Hints for Thin Deep Nets
Patient Knowledge Distillation for BERT Model Compression
MiniLM: Deep Self-Attention Distillation for Task-Agnostic Compression of Pre-Trained Transformers

cases.rationale: Feature-level distillation (FitNets, 2014) directly covers this idea. Intermediate activation supervision for knowledge transfer is a well-established and heavily-cited technique.

cases.idea: A graph neural network that aggregates neighbor features using attention weights derived from the cosine similarity between node embeddings, applied to node classification tasks.

gold_papers.title: Graph Attention Networks
Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks
How Attentive are Graph Attention Networks?
On the Impact of Communities on Semi-supervised Classification Using Graph Neural Networks

cases.rationale: GAT (2017) and GATv2 implement attention-based neighbor aggregation using similarity-based scores. The proposed idea is a well-known baseline.

cases.idea: Training a variational autoencoder where the latent space is disentangled by adding a total correlation penalty term to the ELBO loss, encouraging statistically independent latent dimensions.

gold_papers.title: Isolating Sources of Disentanglement in Variational Autoencoders
Disentangling by Factorising
Challenging Common Assumptions in the Unsupervised Learning of Disentangled Representations

cases.rationale: TC-VAE / FactorVAE (2018) directly proposes total correlation penalization in the ELBO for disentanglement. This idea is fully covered.

cases.idea: A side-channel attack on LLM inference APIs that recovers approximate input token counts by timing the streaming token-generation responses, then uses this to fingerprint the query category without seeing the actual input.

gold_papers.title: Stealing Part of a Production Language Model
Privacy Side Channels in Machine Learning Systems
Defending against Machine Learning based Inference Attacks via Adversarial Examples: Opportunities and Challenges
Scalable Extraction of Training Data from (Production) Language Models

cases.rationale: Timing-based side-channel attacks specific to streaming LLM APIs as a query fingerprinting mechanism have not been systematically studied. This is a novel attack surface.

cases.idea: A network intrusion detection system that uses a hyperbolic embedding space to represent network flow relationships, exploiting hyperbolic geometry's ability to model hierarchical structures inherent in network topology and attack taxonomies.

gold_papers.title: Poincaré Embeddings for Learning Hierarchical Representations
Entente: Cross-silo Intrusion Detection on Network Log Graphs with Federated Learning
Hyperbolic Graph Neural Networks
Knowledge-Augmented Explainable and Interpretable Learning for Anomaly Detection and Diagnosis

cases.rationale: Applying hyperbolic embeddings to NIDS to exploit the hierarchical structure of network topologies and attack trees is a novel framing. Existing NIDS work is Euclidean.

cases.idea: A membership inference attack tailored to federated learning that exploits the gradient update history from a compromised participant, reconstructing whether a specific sample was used in a victim client's local training batch.

gold_papers.title: Comprehensive Privacy Analysis of Deep Learning: Passive and Active White-box Inference Attacks against Centralized and Federated Learning
Inverting Gradients -- How easy is it to break privacy in federated learning?
R-GAP: Recursive Gradient Attack on Privacy
Label Leakage and Protection in Two-party Split Learning

cases.rationale: Using a compromised participant's gradient history (across rounds) rather than a single round's gradients for membership inference is a novel threat model.

cases.idea: Augmenting a transformer-based malware classification model with a domain-specific byte n-gram tokenizer trained on malware corpora rather than using standard BPE tokenization, to better capture executable structure.

gold_papers.title: MalBERT: Using Transformers for Cybersecurity and Malicious Software Detection
Byte Pair Encoding is Suboptimal for Language Model Pretraining
Emotion Detection on TV Show Transcripts with Sequence-based Convolutional Neural Networks
Towards interpreting ML-based automated malware detection models: a survey

cases.rationale: Domain-specific tokenization for malware is a reasonable engineering improvement to existing transformer-based classifiers; the conceptual novelty is limited.

cases.idea: Applying adversarial training with PGD-generated perturbations to a network anomaly detection model to improve its robustness against evasion attacks that craft subtly modified malicious traffic.

gold_papers.title: Towards Deep Learning Models Resistant to Adversarial Attacks
Adversarial Examples in Modern Machine Learning: A Review
Enhancing Robustness Against Adversarial Examples in Network Intrusion Detection Systems

cases.rationale: Applying standard adversarial training methods (PGD) to NIDS robustness is a direct transfer from computer vision; the technique is well-established.

cases.idea: A phishing URL detection system that uses a character-level CNN to extract features from the URL string and combines them with WHOIS metadata features through a late-fusion MLP.

gold_papers.title: URLNet: Learning a URL Representation with Deep Learning for Malicious URL Detection
A Deep Learning Model with Hierarchical LSTMs and Supervised Attention for Anti-Phishing
The Performance of Sequential Deep Learning Models in Detecting Phishing Websites Using Contextual Features of URLs

Risks & Benefits of LLMs & GenAI for Platform Integrity, Healthcare Diagnostics, Financial Trust and Compliance, Cybersecurity, Privacy & AI Safety: A Comprehensive Survey, Roadmap & Implementation Blueprint

cases.rationale: Character-level CNNs for URL classification plus metadata fusion is a widely explored pattern; this combination exists in several prior systems.

cases.idea: A generative adversarial network framework to produce synthetic network intrusion data for augmenting imbalanced intrusion detection datasets, where the discriminator also functions as the final anomaly detector.

gold_papers.title: IDSGAN: Generative Adversarial Networks for Attack Generation against Intrusion Detection
Synthetic Data Augmentation for Medical Audio Classification: A Preliminary Evaluation
$\text{C}^{2}\text{BNVAE}$: Dual-Conditional Deep Generation of Network Traffic Data for Network Intrusion Detection System Balancing
A Review on Generative Adversarial Networks for Data Augmentation in Person Re-Identification Systems

cases.rationale: GAN-based synthetic traffic generation for IDS augmentation with a discriminator-as-detector architecture is directly covered by IDSGAN and related work.

cases.idea: A federated learning approach to train an intrusion detection model across multiple organizations without sharing raw network traffic data, preserving privacy while enabling collaborative learning.

gold_papers.title: A review of Federated Learning in Intrusion Detection Systems for IoT
Towards Adapting Federated & Quantum Machine Learning for Network Intrusion Detection: A Survey
Federated Foundation Models: Privacy-Preserving and Collaborative Learning for Large Models
Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning via Homomorphic Adversarial Networks

cases.rationale: Federated learning for privacy-preserving IDS across organizations is explicitly addressed in multiple surveys and systems papers published since 2020.

cases.idea: Using BERT fine-tuned on security forum text and CVE descriptions to automatically classify vulnerability severity and extract affected software components from unstructured bug reports.

gold_papers.title: VulBERTa: Simplified Source Code Pre-Training for Vulnerability Detection
SecureBERT: A Domain-Specific Language Model for Cybersecurity

cases.rationale: BERT fine-tuning on CVE/security text for vulnerability classification and entity extraction is directly addressed by SecBERT, CVE-NLP, and related systems.

cases.idea: A system that detects deepfake audio in real-time voice calls by training a binary classifier on spectrogram features extracted from short audio segments, distinguishing synthesized from genuine speech.

gold_papers.title: Zero-Shot to Zero-Lies: Detecting Bengali Deepfake Audio through Transfer Learning
Replay and Synthetic Speech Detection with Res2net Architecture
ASVspoof 2021: Towards Spoofed and Deepfake Speech Detection in the Wild

cases.rationale: Spectrogram-based deepfake audio detection is a central problem in the ASVspoof benchmark community with many published approaches predating this idea.

cases.idea: A graph neural network trained on DFT-computed property datasets that predicts elastic tensor components for alloys by encoding crystallographic symmetry constraints directly into the message-passing aggregation rules, rather than learning symmetry implicitly.

gold_papers.title: Crystal Graph Convolutional Neural Networks for an Accurate and Interpretable Prediction of Material Properties
Quantized SO(3)-Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for Efficient Molecular Property Prediction
Probing Elastic Isotropy in Entropy Stabilized Transition Metal Oxides: Experimental Estimation of Single Crystal Elastic Constants from Polycrystalline Materials
EGraFFBench: Evaluation of Equivariant Graph Neural Network Force Fields for Atomistic Simulations

cases.rationale: Encoding crystallographic symmetry constraints explicitly in message-passing aggregation — as distinct from equivariant architectures that learn symmetry — for elastic tensor prediction is unexplored.

cases.idea: A generative model for porous material design that uses a latent diffusion process on voxelized unit cell representations, conditioned on target gas adsorption capacity and thermal stability, enabling inverse design of MOFs.

gold_papers.title: Realizing the Data-Driven, Computational Discovery of Metal-Organic Framework Catalysts
Prediction of typhoon tracks using a generative adversarial network with observational and meteorological data
Diffusion probabilistic models enhance variational autoencoder for crystal structure generative modeling
Generative deep learning for the inverse design of materials

cases.rationale: Applying latent diffusion to voxelized MOF unit cells with dual conditioning on adsorption and thermal stability targets is a novel combination not present in existing generative MOF work.

cases.idea: Extending a graph neural network trained on the Materials Project database to predict band gaps of perovskite oxides by fine-tuning on a smaller targeted perovskite dataset with domain-adaptive layer unfreezing.

gold_papers.title: Regression with Large Language Models for Materials and Molecular Property Prediction
Machine-Learning Prediction of the Computed Band Gaps of Double Perovskite Materials
Graph Networks as a Universal Machine Learning Framework for Molecules and Crystals
Materials Representation and Transfer Learning for Multi-Property Prediction

cases.rationale: Transfer learning on MEGNet or similar GNNs for perovskite properties is a predictable extension; domain-adaptive fine-tuning is a standard technique applied to a known materials prediction setting.

cases.idea: Using active learning with Gaussian process uncertainty to select which alloy compositions to compute via DFT next, reducing the number of calculations needed to map out a binary alloy phase diagram.

gold_papers.title: Foundation-Model Surrogates Enable Data-Efficient Active Learning for Materials Discovery
Gaussian Approximation Potentials: a brief tutorial introduction
Bayesian optimization for materials design
Physics in the Machine: Integrating Physical Knowledge in Autonomous Phase-Mapping

cases.rationale: GP-based active learning for efficient DFT campaign design is a well-established methodology; application to binary alloy phase diagram mapping is incremental.

cases.idea: Applying a variational autoencoder to encode molecular fingerprints of polymer repeat units into a continuous latent space, then using Bayesian optimization in that space to search for polymers with high dielectric constants.

gold_papers.title: Automatic Chemical Design Using a Data-Driven Continuous Representation of Molecules
Polymer Informatics: Current Status and Critical Next Steps
A chemical bond-based machine learning model for dipole moment: Application to dielectric properties of liquid methanol and ethanol
Benchmarking Large Language Models for Polymer Property Predictions

cases.rationale: VAE + BO in molecular latent space is a canonical inverse design pipeline (JT-VAE, JTVAE-BO); applying it to dielectric polymers is an application paper, not a methodological advance.

cases.idea: A convolutional neural network trained on transmission electron microscopy images to automatically identify and classify crystal defect types including vacancies, dislocations, and grain boundaries.

gold_papers.title: Deep Learning based Defect classification and detection in SEM images: A Mask R-CNN approach
Automated Grain Boundary (GB) Segmentation and Microstructural Analysis in 347H Stainless Steel Using Deep Learning and Multimodal Microscopy
The Adoption of Image-Driven Machine Learning for Microstructure Characterization and Materials Design: A Perspective

cases.rationale: CNN-based TEM defect detection and classification is a published, benchmarked task with multiple dedicated systems.

cases.idea: Training a neural network potential using a message-passing architecture on DFT-labeled atomic configurations to simulate molecular dynamics for lithium-ion battery cathode materials without full DFT at each timestep.

gold_papers.title: SchNet: A continuous-filter convolutional neural network for modeling quantum interactions
Machine-learning interatomic potentials for materials science
E(3)-Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for Data-Efficient and Accurate Interatomic Potentials
DeePMD-kit: A deep learning package for many-body potential energy representation and molecular dynamics

cases.rationale: Neural network potentials for battery materials MD simulation is a major active research area with multiple production-level tools (DeePMD, NequIP, MACE); the idea is well-covered.

cases.idea: A random forest model trained on compositional and structural descriptors from the Materials Project to predict whether a given inorganic compound will be thermodynamically stable.

gold_papers.title: Statistical stability indices for LIME: obtaining reliable explanations for Machine Learning models
Predicting the thermodynamic stability of perovskite oxides using machine learning models
Prediction of Mechanical Properties and Thermodynamic Stability of Ti-N system using MTP Interatomic Potential

cases.rationale: ML-based thermodynamic stability prediction from Materials Project descriptors is a textbook application; random forest on compositional features for this task has been published repeatedly.

cases.idea: A contrastive learning framework for learning representations of crystal structures where positive pairs are the same structure viewed under different symmetry-equivalent orientations, and negatives are structures from different space groups, trained to produce space-group-aware embeddings for downstream property prediction.

gold_papers.title: A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations
Crystal Twins: Self-supervised Learning for Crystalline Material Property Prediction
E(3)-Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for Data-Efficient and Accurate Interatomic Potentials
Contrastive Learning of English Language and Crystal Graphs for Multimodal Representation of Materials Knowledge

cases.rationale: Space-group-aware contrastive learning where positive pairs are defined by symmetry equivalence and negatives by space group identity is a specific and novel instantiation not seen in Crystal Twins or adjacent work.

cases.idea: Adding a physically motivated loss term to a GNN-based force field that penalizes predicted forces that violate Newton's third law (action-reaction symmetry) during training, to improve physical consistency of simulations.

gold_papers.title: Equivariant message passing for the prediction of tensorial properties and molecular spectra
Physically-informed artificial neural networks for atomistic modeling of materials
A Systematic Survey and Benchmark of Deep Learning for Molecular Property Prediction in the Foundation Model Era

cases.rationale: Adding Newton's-law-based auxiliary losses to GNN force fields is a reasonable physics-informed training improvement; similar constraints have been explored in adjacent energy-conserving potential work.

cases.idea: A multi-modal transformer that jointly processes whole-slide histopathology image patches and matched RNA-seq gene expression profiles, using cross-attention between modalities to predict cancer subtype and survival prognosis simultaneously.

gold_papers.title: Multimodal Optimal Transport-based Co-Attention Transformer with Global Structure Consistency for Survival Prediction
Multimodal Learning and Cognitive Processes in Radiology: MedGaze for Chest X-ray Scanpath Prediction
Transformer-Based Self-Supervised Learning for Histopathological Classification of Ischemic Stroke Clot Origin
HIGT: Hierarchical Interaction Graph-Transformer for Whole Slide Image Analysis

cases.rationale: Existing multimodal survival work either uses co-attention or cross-attention but not a unified transformer with simultaneous subtype + survival multi-task output from both modalities end-to-end. The joint multi-task framing is novel.

cases.idea: A graph-based drug-drug interaction prediction model that uses patient electronic health records to construct co-prescription networks, treating real-world prescription patterns as weak supervision signals for learning drug compatibility embeddings.

gold_papers.title: CASTER: Predicting Drug Interactions with Chemical Substructure Representation
SafeDrug: Dual Molecular Graph Encoders for Recommending Effective and Safe Drug Combinations
Predicting Rich Drug-Drug Interactions via Biomedical Knowledge Graphs and Text Jointly Embedding
Democratizing Large Language Model-Based Graph Data Augmentation via Latent Knowledge Graphs

cases.rationale: Using real-world EHR co-prescription patterns as weak supervision — rather than curated interaction labels — to train DDI compatibility embeddings is a novel supervision paradigm.

cases.idea: A reinforcement learning agent that learns to design personalized adaptive radiotherapy treatment plans by iteratively adjusting dose fractionation in response to simulated tumor response and healthy tissue recovery dynamics modeled from patient-specific biology.

gold_papers.title: Adaptive Model-Based Reinforcement Learning for Orbit Feedback Control in NSLS-II Storage Ring
A Patient-Specific Digital Twin for Adaptive Radiotherapy of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
Bayesian Nonparametric Estimation for Dynamic Treatment Regimes with Sequential Transition Times
Distributed Fractional Bayesian Learning for Adaptive Optimization

cases.rationale: Integrating patient-specific biological response modeling (tumor + healthy tissue) as the environment dynamics in an RL agent for iterative fractionation adaptation is not yet unified in existing work.

cases.idea: Fine-tuning a pre-trained protein language model (ESM-2) on a labeled dataset of antibiotic resistance mutations to predict whether a point mutation in a bacterial protein confers resistance to a specific antibiotic class.

gold_papers.title: Structure of the space of folding protein sequences defined by large language models
Modeling Protein Using Large-scale Pretrain Language Model
Generating protein sequences from antibiotic resistance genes data using Generative Adversarial Networks
Fine-tuning Protein Language Models with Deep Mutational Scanning improves Variant Effect Prediction

cases.rationale: Fine-tuning ESM-class models for mutation effect prediction is a standard paradigm; antibiotic resistance is a specific application domain.

cases.idea: A semi-supervised learning approach for medical image segmentation that uses a teacher-student consistency loss on unlabeled CT scans to improve performance when labeled training data is scarce.

gold_papers.title: Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation through Dual-task Consistency
Mean teachers are better role models: Weight-averaged consistency targets improve semi-supervised deep learning results
Self-training with Noisy Student improves ImageNet classification
Not-so-supervised: a survey of semi-supervised, multi-instance, and transfer learning in medical image analysis

cases.rationale: Mean-teacher consistency training for semi-supervised medical image segmentation is well-established; this is a direct application of known techniques to CT segmentation.

cases.idea: A clinical NLP pipeline that uses a domain-adapted BERT model to extract social determinants of health (housing instability, food insecurity) from unstructured clinical notes in EHR systems.

gold_papers.title: BioBERT: A Pre-trained Biomedical Language Representation Model
Harnessing the Power of BERT in the Turkish Clinical Domain: Pretraining Approaches for Limited Data Scenarios
Task formulation for Extracting Social Determinants of Health from Clinical Narratives
Synth-SBDH: A Synthetic Dataset of Social and Behavioral Determinants of Health for Clinical Text

cases.rationale: BERT-based SDOH extraction from clinical notes is directly addressed in multiple published systems; the methodological contribution is incremental.

cases.idea: A U-Net-based architecture for automatic segmentation of brain tumors in multi-modal MRI scans (T1, T2, FLAIR, T1ce), trained and evaluated on the BraTS benchmark dataset.

gold_papers.title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
Brain Tumor Segmentation and Radiomics Survival Prediction
nnU-Net: Self-adapting Framework for U-Net-Based Medical Image Segmentation
TransUNet: Transformers Make Strong Encoders for Medical Image Segmentation

cases.rationale: Multi-modal MRI brain tumor segmentation with U-Net on BraTS is one of the most-published benchmark tasks in medical imaging. Fully covered.

cases.idea: Using a recurrent neural network (LSTM) trained on time-series EHR data (vitals, labs, medications) to predict in-hospital mortality for ICU patients.

gold_papers.title: Doctor AI: Predicting Clinical Events via Recurrent Neural Networks
Multitask learning and benchmarking with clinical time series data
Benchmark of Deep Learning Models on Large Healthcare MIMIC Datasets
Clinical Outcome Prediction from Admission Notes using Self-Supervised Knowledge Integration

cases.rationale: LSTM-based ICU mortality prediction from EHR time series is a canonical deep health benchmark task, addressed since at least 2016 (Doctor AI).

cases.idea: A transfer learning approach using a ResNet-50 pre-trained on ImageNet, fine-tuned for diabetic retinopathy grading from fundus photographs, evaluated on the EyePACS and Messidor-2 datasets.

gold_papers.title: Replication study: Development and validation of deep learning algorithm for detection of diabetic retinopathy in retinal fundus photographs
Regression and Learning with Pixel-wise Attention for Retinal Fundus Glaucoma Segmentation and Detection
A Systematic Comparison of Bayesian Deep Learning Robustness in Diabetic Retinopathy Tasks

cases.rationale: ImageNet pre-trained CNN fine-tuning for DR grading is one of the earliest and most saturated deep medical imaging applications, covered by Google's landmark 2016 paper and countless subsequent works.

cases.idea: Applying AlphaFold2-predicted protein structures as input features for a machine learning model that predicts protein-protein interaction binding affinity.

gold_papers.title: Accurate Protein Structure Prediction by Embeddings and Deep Learning Representations
Predicting disease-related genes by path-based similarity and community structure in protein-protein interaction network
Improved Protein-ligand Binding Affinity Prediction with Structure-Based Deep Fusion Inference

cases.rationale: Using AlphaFold2 structures as input for PPI binding affinity ML models has been directly published and benchmarked in multiple papers since 2022.
